content.text: I'm going to be giving you a full
tutorial on how you can grow your
YouTube channel by using what are called
infeed ads. These are ads that appear in
search results as suggested videos
alongside other videos and also as
videos that pop up in the home feed
right there on the homepage of YouTube.
If you have a catalog of YouTube videos
that you've been producing, maybe
producing a variety of different videos,
and you want to get more people to see
this content, what you can do is use
this exact tutorial that I'm going to be
showing you right here today in order to
actually get more exposure for those
videos using infeed ads. And this allows
you to grow your organic reach on
YouTube and actually can help you
snowball your channel by increasing your
overall watch time and having you appear
in more organic search results, reach
new people, and gain new subscribers.
And here's the best part. Infeed ads are
a fraction of the cost of regular
instream ads. What we have found is that
infeed ads are typically about 1/5 to
onetenth the cost per view as instream
ads, ads that appear in front of other
videos on YouTube. You might be
wondering why that is. Well, it's
because YouTube wants to keep people on
the platform. And they're going to
charge you a premium if you're getting
people off the platform with instream
ads versus infeed ads that keep people
consuming more and more YouTube content.
Also, a lot less people use infeed ads
versus instream. So, this is a big
opportunity for you to be able to grow
your channel and get in front of your
ideal clients. Let's hop over to my
computer screen here and I'm going to
show you how you could promote any of
your existing YouTube videos using these
infeed ads. Now, the first thing you're
going to need is, of course, your main
YouTube account. And then you're going
to need to link that to a Google Ads
account. You can go to ads.google.com
and sign up for your very own Google Ads
account. Now, once you have a new Google
Ads account, you've set up all of your
billing and details, you're going to
want to link your YouTube channel to
that ads account. The way to do that is
to go down over here to the admin bar.
So, you can see we click on admin. Then,
we click on linked accounts. Then, we go
over to YouTube and we click manage and
link. Then, we click this big blue plus
button. You put in the URL for your
YouTube channel and then follow the
steps that will have you link your
channel. Now, make sure to give full
permissions on view counts, remarketing,
and engagement. And that's going to
allow you to link your YouTube channel
to your YouTube ad account. It's going
to allow you to run YouTube ads directly
towards your videos. Then from there,
what you're going to do is you're going
to go over here to click plus create and
then new campaign. Go over here and
you'll see your objective. Now, normally
with instream ads, we might create a
leadbased ad, but instead what we're
going to create is what's called an
awareness and consideration campaign.
This allows us to reach brand new people
with your YouTube video. So, we're going
to click awareness and consideration.
And then we're going to go over to
video, reach viewers on YouTube and get
conversions. And then you can see here
that there's actually a campaign subtype
called video views. Now, this is a newer
subtype. In the past, it's been more of
a video reach direct campaign. We're
going to do this as a video views
campaign. And we're going to go ahead
and we're going to click continue. Now,
once we create that, we're going to want
to set up our naming convention here
inside of YouTube. So, I always like to
start with the date. So, 10 1023. Then
we talk about what the objective is. So,
in this case, it's going to be uh video
views. And then we're going to choose
what is the particular video that we're

we wanted to promote this video, which I
